name: recruitcrm-mcp-server
description: Search candidates, track pipelines, & manage your recruiting workflow
long_description: Access candidates, jobs, companies, or contacts - dig into notes, tasks, meetings, call logs, and hiring stages. Build hotlists, track pipeline activity, and get up to speed before any candidate or client interaction.

api_token.title: Recruit CRM API Token
api_token.description: Your Recruit CRM API token (found in Admin Settings → API)

tools.name: search_candidates
tools.description: Search candidates with filters and return compact summaries. For 'my' candidates, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id. Opt-in include_contact_info flag adds email, contact_number, and linkedin to results.

tools.name: list_candidates
tools.description: List all candidates with pagination/sorting only (no filter required). For 'my' candidates, use search_candidates with owner_id. Opt-in include_contact_info flag adds email, contact_number, and linkedin to results.

tools.name: search_jobs
tools.description: Search jobs with filters and return compact summaries. For 'my' jobs, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id.

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: List all jobs with pagination/sorting only (no filter required). For 'my jobs', use search_jobs with owner_id.

tools.name: search_companies
tools.description: Search companies with filters and return compact summaries. For 'my' companies, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id.

tools.name: list_companies
tools.description: List all companies with pagination/sorting only (no filter required). For 'my' companies, use search_companies with owner_id.

tools.name: search_contacts
tools.description: Search contacts with filters and return compact summaries. For 'my' contacts, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id. Requires at least one real filter; sort/page/exact_search alone do not count. Opt-in include_contact_info adds email, contact_number, and linkedin.

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List all contacts with pagination/sorting only (no filter required). For 'my' contacts, use search_contacts with owner_id. Opt-in include_contact_info adds email, contact_number, and linkedin.

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: List Recruit CRM users with compact id, first_name, last_name, and status fields. Opt-in include_teams adds team memberships; opt-in include_contact_info adds email and contact_number.

tools.name: search_hotlists
tools.description: Search hotlists by required related_to_type and optional name/shared filters. Does not support owner filters; do not invent owner_id for 'my' requests. Broad searches return related_count only; name-targeted searches also include related_slugs.

tools.name: create_hotlist
tools.description: Create one hotlist. Requires created_by user id and shared 0/1. This tool modifies Recruit CRM data and should only be used when explicitly requested.

tools.name: add_records_to_hotlist
tools.description: Add up to 10 record slugs to an existing hotlist. This tool modifies Recruit CRM data, runs sequentially, ignores duplicate input slugs, and returns partial success details.

tools.name: search_tasks
tools.description: Search tasks and return compact summaries with related entity context. For 'my' tasks, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id.

tools.name: list_task_types
tools.description: List task types with id and label. Use before create_task to resolve task_type_id from a requested type label.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create one Recruit CRM task. Requires task_type_id, title, description, reminder, start_date, owner_id, and created_by. Use list_task_types first and list_users for owner/creator resolution; supports basic HTML/rich text in description. This tool modifies Recruit CRM data and returns compact output.

tools.name: search_meetings
tools.description: Search meetings and return compact summaries with scheduling metadata. For 'my' meetings, resolve the Recruit CRM user id via list_users and filter by owner_id.

tools.name: search_notes
tools.description: Search notes and return compact summaries with related entity context. Does not support owner filters; do not invent owner_id for 'my' requests.

tools.name: list_note_types
tools.description: List note types with id and label. Use before create_note to resolve note_type_id from a requested type label.

tools.name: create_note
tools.description: Create one Recruit CRM note. Requires note_type_id, created_by user id, related entity, and description. Use list_note_types first; supports basic HTML/rich text in description. This tool modifies Recruit CRM data and returns compact output.

tools.name: search_call_logs
tools.description: Search call logs and return compact summaries with related entity context. Does not support owner filters; do not invent owner_id for 'my' requests.

tools.name: get_candidate_details
tools.description: Fetch full details for up to 10 candidates by slug in parallel. If ownership matters, first use owner-scoped search_candidates. Returns partial results with a separate errors array for failed slugs.

tools.name: get_job_details
tools.description: Fetch one job by slug and return the raw Recruit CRM payload. If ownership matters, first use owner-scoped search_jobs.

tools.name: get_company_details
tools.description: Fetch full details for up to 10 companies by slug in parallel. If ownership matters, first use owner-scoped search_companies. Returns partial results with a separate errors array for failed slugs.

tools.name: get_contact_details
tools.description: Fetch full details for up to 10 contacts by slug in parallel. If ownership matters, first use owner-scoped search_contacts. Returns partial results with a separate errors array for failed slugs.

tools.name: get_job_assigned_candidates
tools.description: Fetch candidates assigned to a job with compact assignment summaries. For 'my job', first resolve the job through owner-scoped search_jobs.

tools.name: list_candidate_hiring_stages
tools.description: List candidate hiring stage labels and ids.

tools.name: list_job_statuses
tools.description: List job pipeline statuses (Open, Closed, On Hold, plus any custom labels) with id and label for resolving status names to the numeric job_status_id used by search_jobs.job_status.

tools.name: get_candidate_job_assignment_hiring_stage_history
tools.description: Fetch a candidate's job assignment hiring stage history.

tools.name: list_candidate_custom_fields
tools.description: List searchable candidate custom field metadata.

tools.name: get_candidate_custom_field_details
tools.description: Fetch details for one candidate custom field.

tools.name: analyze_job_pipeline
tools.description: Diagnose a single job's hiring pipeline in one call: stage distribution, days-in-current-stage, idle candidates, bottleneck verdict, recent notes/meetings/tasks, and suggested next actions. Optionally compute time-to-hire / time-to-stage / time-to-first-action by setting include_time_metrics=true (extra /history calls; default false keeps the call cheap at ~7 API hits). For 'my' jobs, resolve user_id via list_users then filter search_jobs by owner_id before calling this.
